item.title: Firefighters Quickly Contain Ropewalk Lane Grass Fire in Capital
item.dc:creator: Greg Smith
item.description:
Oh my ol' cock, you won't believe what went down this Saturday in St. John's. Somebody started a grass fire behind Power's Salvage on Ropewalk Lane, but don't worry, the fire folks showed up quicker than you can say "holy mackerel!" They had it all sorted before it could roast anything important.
Some smart cookie out for a stroll spotted the smoke and raised the alarm. The smoke was making itself at home, even wafting over Sobeys and the NLC. Imagine that, a fire causing more drama than a night out in George Street!
Thankfully, no harm was done to the Power's Salvage building. Acting Captain Tony Kieley said that in this hot and dry mess of weather, any fire’s a big worry. Aye, Tony, we surely don’t want a bitta flame ruining all the fun, especially since there's enough hot air in politics to go around.
item.content:encoded:
Oh my ol' cock, you won't believe what went down this Saturday in St. John's. Somebody started a grass fire behind Power's Salvage on Ropewalk Lane, but don't worry, the fire folks showed up quicker than you can say "holy mackerel!" They had it all sorted before it could roast anything important.
Some smart cookie out for a stroll spotted the smoke and raised the alarm. The smoke was making itself at home, even wafting over Sobeys and the NLC. Imagine that, a fire causing more drama than a night out in George Street!
Thankfully, no harm was done to the Power's Salvage building. Acting Captain Tony Kieley said that in this hot and dry mess of weather, any fire’s a big worry. Aye, Tony, we surely don’t want a bitta flame ruining all the fun, especially since there's enough hot air in politics to go around.

item.title: RCMP Evacuating Cabins as Forest Fire Burns Near Hickman’s Harbour
item.dc:creator: Greg Smith
item.description: Well, b'ys, looks like the Clarenville RCMP got their hands full with a forest fire 'round Hickman's Harbour. They're shooing folks outta their cabins just to be safe. I s'pose the cops know what they're doing, but it wouldn't surprise me if someone left a pot of beans on the stove too long and it all went up in flames! Anyway, they want everyone to steer clear so the fire folks can do their thing. Just our luck, eh? VOCM's on it, so keep yer ears on for updates. Hope my ol' fishin' spot's not in the blaze’s way, lard tunderin'!
item.content:encoded: Well, b'ys, looks like the Clarenville RCMP got their hands full with a forest fire 'round Hickman's Harbour. They're shooing folks outta their cabins just to be safe. I s'pose the cops know what they're doing, but it wouldn't surprise me if someone left a pot of beans on the stove too long and it all went up in flames! Anyway, they want everyone to steer clear so the fire folks can do their thing. Just our luck, eh? VOCM's on it, so keep yer ears on for updates. Hope my ol' fishin' spot's not in the blaze’s way, lard tunderin'!

item.title: New CEO Kelly Smith Takes Helm at Port of Corner Brook

media:content.media:description: Corner Brook Port Authority CEO Kelly Smith (Submitted)

item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Oh b'y, listen to this. So there's a new captain at the helm of the Port of Corner Brook—Kelly Smith's her name. She's taken over from Jackie Chow who must have been running the show since the days of Moby Dick. Her big plan, it seems, is to keep up all the friendships with the current businesses and maybe shack up with some new ones, 'specially those folks making all the fuss about green energy.
I guess she's got the port folks already buildin' a brand-new warehouse, 'cause you know, you can't just have your hydrogen and whatnot sittin' in a rusty old shed. So they're yappin' with companies about how to roll out the welcome mat for all these big hydrogen projects. Looks like they’re steering the ship towards a more eco-friendly harbor... or at least trying not to get left behind in the dust. Hope they can figure it all out before we all turn into polar bears!
item.content:encoded:
Oh b'y, listen to this. So there's a new captain at the helm of the Port of Corner Brook—Kelly Smith's her name. She's taken over from Jackie Chow who must have been running the show since the days of Moby Dick. Her big plan, it seems, is to keep up all the friendships with the current businesses and maybe shack up with some new ones, 'specially those folks making all the fuss about green energy.
I guess she's got the port folks already buildin' a brand-new warehouse, 'cause you know, you can't just have your hydrogen and whatnot sittin' in a rusty old shed. So they're yappin' with companies about how to roll out the welcome mat for all these big hydrogen projects. Looks like they’re steering the ship towards a more eco-friendly harbor... or at least trying not to get left behind in the dust. Hope they can figure it all out before we all turn into polar bears!

item.title: PC Leader Commits to New MRI for Labrador if Elected
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Well, b'ys, Tony Wakeham, the head honcho of the Progressive Conservatives, is over in Labrador makin' some big promises. He's sayin' he'll plunk down a brand spankin' new MRI machine right there in Labrador, with folks to run it and everything. Apparently, he's not too keen on the current plan, which is just addin' a couple more over in St. John's, as if that’ll help everyone out here in the bay.
On top of that, Wakeham's sayin' he'll cover travel costs for anyone who needs to head off for crucial medical treatments. Sounds like he wants to give folks a break from tryin' to pay their way across the rock just to see a doctor.
He reckons that this could help cut down on those dreadful wait times and ease the hit to everyone’s wallets when they’re already feelin' poorly. Seems pretty decent if you ask me, but we’ll see if it’s all talk or if he actually does any of it.
Anyhow, the clock's tickin' with an election comin' up by mid-October, so we'll soon find out if he's all hat and no cattle, as the sayin' goes.
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'ys, Tony Wakeham, the head honcho of the Progressive Conservatives, is over in Labrador makin' some big promises. He's sayin' he'll plunk down a brand spankin' new MRI machine right there in Labrador, with folks to run it and everything. Apparently, he's not too keen on the current plan, which is just addin' a couple more over in St. John's, as if that’ll help everyone out here in the bay.
On top of that, Wakeham's sayin' he'll cover travel costs for anyone who needs to head off for crucial medical treatments. Sounds like he wants to give folks a break from tryin' to pay their way across the rock just to see a doctor.
He reckons that this could help cut down on those dreadful wait times and ease the hit to everyone’s wallets when they’re already feelin' poorly. Seems pretty decent if you ask me, but we’ll see if it’s all talk or if he actually does any of it.
Anyhow, the clock's tickin' with an election comin' up by mid-October, so we'll soon find out if he's all hat and no cattle, as the sayin' goes.

item.title: Energy Portfolio Vacancy Creates Uncertainty for NL Oil Industry
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Well, b'ys, we've got ourselves a new minister of energy, and it looks like the poor fella's got quite the mountain to climb. Andrew Parsons, who's been running the show for a spell, decided to jump ship, leaving Steve Crocker to pick up the pieces. So, we've got no seasoned skipper, just as we're headin' into an election.
Rob Strong, a chap who's been around the oil block a time or two, is frettin' about a couple of things—like figuring out a good deal for Bay du Nord and cracking the code on gettin' our natural gas off the shores. He's right enough sayin' that the industry just wants some certainties, and who can blame them?
Let's hope whoever ends up with the gig knows their stuff because oil's a slippery business, and these ain't small potatoes we're dealin' with. Good luck to 'em, they'll need it!
item.content:encoded:
Well, b'ys, we've got ourselves a new minister of energy, and it looks like the poor fella's got quite the mountain to climb. Andrew Parsons, who's been running the show for a spell, decided to jump ship, leaving Steve Crocker to pick up the pieces. So, we've got no seasoned skipper, just as we're headin' into an election.
Rob Strong, a chap who's been around the oil block a time or two, is frettin' about a couple of things—like figuring out a good deal for Bay du Nord and cracking the code on gettin' our natural gas off the shores. He's right enough sayin' that the industry just wants some certainties, and who can blame them?
Let's hope whoever ends up with the gig knows their stuff because oil's a slippery business, and these ain't small potatoes we're dealin' with. Good luck to 'em, they'll need it!

item.title: Seven in Ten Small Businesses Disrupted by Construction, Says Report
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Oh b'y, you wouldn't believe the rigmarole goin' on with this construction business. They're doin' up the roads and the underground bits, which is all fine and dandy, but it's givin' everyone a right headache—not just the poor b'ys tryin' to drive through the mess, but the business fellas too. Sure, seven outta ten small businesses are sayin' they're feelin' the pinch 'cause of these diggin' projects. The CFIB put out those numbers this week.
Now this Jonathan Galgay fellow, who's keepin' an eye on things for the CFIB around here, is makin' a big stink about how the towns can't give a bit of a handout to the businesses losin' dough when the street's all dug up. He's got his hands in the air askin' the provincial gov to pull up their britches and change the rules, says it’s already happenin' in the likes of Montreal and Calgary. Apparently, they’ve got these fancy construction mitigation policies where a business can go cap in hand for some compensation. I s'pose it's about time we got on that bandwagon too, eh? But I'll believe it when I sees it.
item.content:encoded:
Oh b'y, you wouldn't believe the rigmarole goin' on with this construction business. They're doin' up the roads and the underground bits, which is all fine and dandy, but it's givin' everyone a right headache—not just the poor b'ys tryin' to drive through the mess, but the business fellas too. Sure, seven outta ten small businesses are sayin' they're feelin' the pinch 'cause of these diggin' projects. The CFIB put out those numbers this week.
Now this Jonathan Galgay fellow, who's keepin' an eye on things for the CFIB around here, is makin' a big stink about how the towns can't give a bit of a handout to the businesses losin' dough when the street's all dug up. He's got his hands in the air askin' the provincial gov to pull up their britches and change the rules, says it’s already happenin' in the likes of Montreal and Calgary. Apparently, they’ve got these fancy construction mitigation policies where a business can go cap in hand for some compensation. I s'pose it's about time we got on that bandwagon too, eh? But I'll believe it when I sees it.

item.title: New Mountain Bike Park for Canada Summer Games Opens Today

media:content.media:description: European Cyclists' Federation / CC

item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description: Well, b'ys, here's a yarn for ya: they've gone and opened up some fancy-dancy bike park in St. John's for the 2025 Canada Summer Games. It's all official-like today. They're callin' it the Volkswagen Audi St. John's Mountain Bike Skills Park, and it's supposed to be the place to be if you're a bike ridin' fella. All the proper bells 'n' whistles, or so they say—enough to take ya from wobbly legs to thinking you're the next Lance Armstrong or somethin'. It's all happenin' over at Pippy Park this mornin'. I guess now we know why we can't find a decent parking spot in town these days—it's all bikes and no b'ys! Cheers to 'em, though—at least someone's gettin' exercise!
item.content:encoded: Well, b'ys, here's a yarn for ya: they've gone and opened up some fancy-dancy bike park in St. John's for the 2025 Canada Summer Games. It's all official-like today. They're callin' it the Volkswagen Audi St. John's Mountain Bike Skills Park, and it's supposed to be the place to be if you're a bike ridin' fella. All the proper bells 'n' whistles, or so they say—enough to take ya from wobbly legs to thinking you're the next Lance Armstrong or somethin'. It's all happenin' over at Pippy Park this mornin'. I guess now we know why we can't find a decent parking spot in town these days—it's all bikes and no b'ys! Cheers to 'em, though—at least someone's gettin' exercise!

item.title: Backyard Concert in Hearts Delight-Islington to Support Autism Society
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Well b'y, there's a knee-slapper of a shindig goin' on in Heart’s Delight-Islington today. The Snelgrove crew have decided to put off a backyard concert at their place on 100 Country Road, and it's all fer a good cause – raisin' money for the Autism Society. They'll be kickin' things off around half-past noon and keepin' it goin' 'til 8 in the evenin' so folks can have a good natter and maybe even a kitchen party in the yard.
Got a real motley crew of local musicians lined up, includin' Rodney Slade, Blair Lane, Trevor Smith & Adya Sequeria, and a band with a name that sounds like somethin' out of a telly-drama, Rev. Dave and the Sin Eaters. Not to mention, good ol' Brad Simmons with some proper country tunes. They're teasin' a surprise performance too; hopeful it ain't Uncle Joe after a few too many screech-ins, tryin' to sing "I'se the B'y."
They got the grill goin' with hot dogs and burgers, and they're askin' folks to donate what they can for admission and grub. All the money's goin' to the Autism Society, so it's a fine cause to get behind. Mind you, if it weren't already such a righteous reason, I might be thinkin' the Snelgroves are pullin' a fast one just to host the biggest backyard bash in Heart’s Delight!
item.content:encoded:
Well b'y, there's a knee-slapper of a shindig goin' on in Heart’s Delight-Islington today. The Snelgrove crew have decided to put off a backyard concert at their place on 100 Country Road, and it's all fer a good cause – raisin' money for the Autism Society. They'll be kickin' things off around half-past noon and keepin' it goin' 'til 8 in the evenin' so folks can have a good natter and maybe even a kitchen party in the yard.
Got a real motley crew of local musicians lined up, includin' Rodney Slade, Blair Lane, Trevor Smith & Adya Sequeria, and a band with a name that sounds like somethin' out of a telly-drama, Rev. Dave and the Sin Eaters. Not to mention, good ol' Brad Simmons with some proper country tunes. They're teasin' a surprise performance too; hopeful it ain't Uncle Joe after a few too many screech-ins, tryin' to sing "I'se the B'y."
They got the grill goin' with hot dogs and burgers, and they're askin' folks to donate what they can for admission and grub. All the money's goin' to the Autism Society, so it's a fine cause to get behind. Mind you, if it weren't already such a righteous reason, I might be thinkin' the Snelgroves are pullin' a fast one just to host the biggest backyard bash in Heart’s Delight!

item.title: RNC Seize Fentanyl, Cocaine in West End Arrest
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Well now, b'ys, it looks like a couple of young eejits got themselves in a right bit of trouble over on the Northeast Avalon, eh? Ol' Martin Marks and Emma Murphy, not even out of their twenties, were nabbed by the RNC after they went and did a drug deal on Blackmarsh Road. Shame they didn't have the good sense to do it out by the woods where nobody'd see 'em, eh?
They had quite the collection, I tell ya—cars, a fake gun, prescription pills, and enough drugs to make ya see double. We're talkin’ magic mushrooms, LSD, and all the hard stuff like crystal meth and fentanyl. Even threw in some contraband smokes and cash for good measure. Can't say I'm surprised they got caught; if you're gonna swim with the sharks, you best learn to paddle, right?
Anyway, they're cooling their heels in the lockup now. I guess they'll have some time to think about their poor life choices. Me? I'm just gonna stick to me Jiggs' dinner and a cuppa tea, thanks very much.
item.content:encoded:
Well now, b'ys, it looks like a couple of young eejits got themselves in a right bit of trouble over on the Northeast Avalon, eh? Ol' Martin Marks and Emma Murphy, not even out of their twenties, were nabbed by the RNC after they went and did a drug deal on Blackmarsh Road. Shame they didn't have the good sense to do it out by the woods where nobody'd see 'em, eh?
They had quite the collection, I tell ya—cars, a fake gun, prescription pills, and enough drugs to make ya see double. We're talkin’ magic mushrooms, LSD, and all the hard stuff like crystal meth and fentanyl. Even threw in some contraband smokes and cash for good measure. Can't say I'm surprised they got caught; if you're gonna swim with the sharks, you best learn to paddle, right?
Anyway, they're cooling their heels in the lockup now. I guess they'll have some time to think about their poor life choices. Me? I'm just gonna stick to me Jiggs' dinner and a cuppa tea, thanks very much.

item.title: Search for Missing Ferry Passenger Handed Over to RCMP
item.dc:creator: Beth Fagan
item.description:
Well, buddy, seems like we've got another mystery on our hands here. A 41-year-old woman named Ping Chang has gone missing after taking the ferry from North Sydney. The search and rescue folks were out there doing their best, but now they're passing the baton over to the RCMP. I guess they're hoping for some land-lubber expertise to crack this one.
Chang was last spotted on the Blue Puttees ferry at the ungodly hour of 3:30 in the morning last Wednesday. Now the Mounties are asking anyone with a set of eyes who might have seen her to give 'em a call. If you happen to know anything, don't be shy and give them a ring at 709-695-2149.
Hopefully they sort it out soon, 'cause it's never a good time when someone just ups and vanishes. But honestly, with all those security cameras and whatnot, you'd think they'd have a clue by now! Here's hopin' they find her safe and sound.
item.content:encoded:
Well, buddy, seems like we've got another mystery on our hands here. A 41-year-old woman named Ping Chang has gone missing after taking the ferry from North Sydney. The search and rescue folks were out there doing their best, but now they're passing the baton over to the RCMP. I guess they're hoping for some land-lubber expertise to crack this one.
Chang was last spotted on the Blue Puttees ferry at the ungodly hour of 3:30 in the morning last Wednesday. Now the Mounties are asking anyone with a set of eyes who might have seen her to give 'em a call. If you happen to know anything, don't be shy and give them a ring at 709-695-2149.
Hopefully they sort it out soon, 'cause it's never a good time when someone just ups and vanishes. But honestly, with all those security cameras and whatnot, you'd think they'd have a clue by now! Here's hopin' they find her safe and sound.
